How dependent is Skyworks Solutions on Apple as a customer, according to its fiscal year 2024 10-K?

golden_citations.key_phrase: constituted more than ten percent of our net revenue

items.golden_answer: A small number of OEMs historically has accounted for a significant portion of our net revenue. In each of the fiscal years ended September 27, 2024 (fiscal 2024), September 29, 2023 (fiscal 2023), and September 30, 2022 (fiscal 2022), Apple, through sales to multiple distributors and contract manufacturers for multiple applications including smartphones, tablets, desktop and notebook computers, watches, and other devices, constituted more than ten percent of our net revenue. Further, the Company's three largest accounts receivable balances comprised 80% and 83% of aggregate gross accounts receivable as of September 27, 2024 and September 29, 2023, respectively.

items.question: What end markets and applications does Skyworks Solutions serve?

golden_citations.key_phrase: aerospace, automotive, broadband, cellular infrastructure, connected home, defense, entertainment and gaming, industrial, medical, smartphone, tablet, and wearables

items.golden_answer: Skyworks Solutions, Inc., together with its consolidated subsidiaries (Skyworks or the Company), is a leading developer, manufacturer and provider of analog and mixed-signal semiconductor products and solutions for numerous applications, including aerospace, automotive, broadband, cellular infrastructure, connected home, defense, entertainment and gaming, industrial, medical, smartphone, tablet, and wearables.

items.question: How does Broadcom describe its history and the role of the VMware acquisition?

golden_citations.key_phrase: 60-year history of innovation dates back to our diverse origins from AT&T/Bell Labs

items.golden_answer: We are a global technology leader that designs, develops and supplies a broad range of semiconductor and semiconductor-based solutions and infrastructure software solutions. Our more than 60-year history of innovation dates back to our diverse origins from AT&T/Bell Labs, Lucent and Hewlett-Packard Company, and has evolved through acquisitions, including LSI Corporation, Broadcom Corporation, Brocade Communications Systems, Inc., CA, Inc., Symantec Enterprise Security, and VMware, Inc. (VMware). Over the years, we have assembled a large team of semiconductor and software design engineers around the world.

items.question: What product and service categories does Apple report revenue from in its 10-K?

golden_citations.key_phrase: designs, manufactures and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables and accessories

items.golden_answer: The Company designs, manufactures and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables and accessories, and sells a variety of related services. Hardware products: iPhone (smartphones based on iOS), Mac (personal computers based on macOS, including MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, iMac, Mac mini, Mac Studio, Mac Pro), iPad (multipurpose tablets based on iPadOS), and Wearables, Home and Accessories (smartwatches, wireless headphones, spatial computers). Services: Advertising (the Company's own advertising platforms), AppleCare (fee-based service and support products), Cloud Services, and Digital Content (App Store, Apple Arcade, Apple Fitness+, and other subscription-based services).

items.question: How does Apple describe its AppleCare service offerings in its 10-K?

golden_citations.key_phrase: portfolio of fee-based service and support products under the AppleCare

items.golden_answer: The Company offers a portfolio of fee-based service and support products under the AppleCare brand. The offerings provide priority access to Apple technical support, access to the global Apple authorized service network for repair and replacement services, and in many cases additional coverage for instances of accidental damage or theft and loss, depending on the country and type of product.

items.question: What does Qorvo's 10-K say about customer concentration risk?

golden_citations.key_phrase: accounted for 46% and 37% of total revenue in fiscal years 2024 and 2023

items.golden_answer: We provide products to our largest end customer, Apple Inc. ('Apple'), through sales to multiple contract manufacturers, which in the aggregate accounted for 46% and 37% of total revenue in fiscal years 2024 and 2023, respectively. Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. ('Samsung') accounted for 12% of total revenue in both fiscal years 2024 and 2023. These customers primarily purchase RF solutions for a variety of mobile devices.

items.question: What are Corning's main business segments described in its 10-K?

golden_citations.key_phrase: The Optical Communications segment is divided into two main product groupi

items.golden_answer: Corning operates through five reportable segments. Optical Communications: fiber and connectivity solutions for telecommunications carrier networks and enterprise/data center networks. Display Technologies: glass substrates for flat panel LCDs and OLEDs used in televisions, notebook computers, monitors, tablets and handheld devices; manufacturing operations in China, South Korea and Taiwan. Specialty Materials: Gorilla Glass for mobile devices and semiconductor optics (ultra-flat glass wafers for advanced semiconductor packaging, 3D sensing, and augmented reality). Environmental Technologies: automotive and diesel emission control products. Life Sciences: laboratory equipment and vessels.

items.question: What risk factors does Skyworks identify related to reliance on a small number of customers?

golden_citations.key_phrase: Significant portions of our sales are concentrated among a limited number of customers

items.golden_answer: Significant portions of our sales are concentrated among a limited number of customers. If we lost one or more of these major customers, or if one or more major customers significantly decreased its orders for our products, our business, results of operations, and financial condition could be materially and adversely impacted, which could adversely affect our stock price. In each of fiscal 2023, fiscal 2022, and fiscal 2021, one customer accounted for greater than ten percent of our net revenue.
